primary_scenes.title: Primary manifest scene
primary_scenes.focus_rule: Return to the manifest sidebar row, canvas anchor, or inspector trigger that opened support detail.
recovery_rules: Scene restoration never reopens a manifest after tenant switch, privilege downgrade, or masking drift.
Resize or inspector collapse must preserve the same manifest scene identity, dominant question, and settlement posture.

source_refs.rationale: The primary split-view scene is explicit.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Native secondary windows and preview subjects

primary_scenes.title: Primary work-item scene
primary_scenes.focus_rule: Return to the invoking sidebar item, selected module anchor, or inspector trigger.
recovery_rules: Reconnect and rebase must match the browser collaboration contract.
Scene restoration keeps the same work item, dominant question, and settlement posture when still legal.

source_refs.rationale: Work-item data flow and cursor/resume model are explicit.
Rebase, stale-view, and scene-identity parity are explicit acceptance criteria.

secondary_windows.title: Detached compare window
secondary_windows.focus_rule: Return to the exact parent compare trigger or selected evidence row.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows

source_refs.rationale: Preview subject legality and temp-artifact purges are explicit.

secondary_windows.title: Detached audit window
secondary_windows.focus_rule: Return to the parent audit trigger or provenance row anchor.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows

source_refs.rationale: Quick Look and export legality are explicit.

secondary_windows.title: Detached filing-packet window
secondary_windows.focus_rule: Return to the parent packet review trigger or selected evidence row.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Filing packet review/export windows are explicit.
Temporary artifact purge rules are explicit.

secondary_windows.title: Detached authority review window
secondary_windows.focus_rule: Return to the parent authority review trigger.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows

source_refs.rationale: Authority work still uses governed browser/session flows when needed.

auth_handoff.resume_rule: Completion never implies settlement until the governed parent read model refreshes and the return target is still legal.

source_refs.rationale: External handoff returns to the same shell.
Persistence and purge rules are explicit.

performance_strategy: virtualization for long evidence or audit lists
translation_notes: Browser routes become `NavigationSplitView`, `WindowGroup`, and dedicated support windows.
Refresh becomes snapshot hydration plus stream resume/rebase rather than browser reload.
Native support windows remain embodiments of calm-shell support law, not a new shell family.
